steps.title: Turn the g table into an f table
steps.content:
The table gives $g(x)$, but the question asks for the y-intercept of $y=f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\dfrac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each table output by $x+3$ to get $f(x)$:
  • $f(-21)=2(-21+3)=-36$
  • $f(-9)=0(-9+3)=0$
  • $f(15)=4(15+3)=72$

steps.title: Find the linear function f
steps.content: Use two of the converted points, $(-9,0)$ and $(15,72)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{72-0}{15-(-9)}=\frac{72}{24}=3.$$ Since $f(-9)=0$, use $f(x)=3x+b$:$$0=3(-9)+b\Rightarrow b=27$$So $f(x)=3x+27$.

steps.title: Identify the intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$. From $f(x)=3x+27$, the intercept is $(0,27)$. D is correct.
